message:
Close sqlmodel_rust-6vd epic: Query Builder Layer complete

All success criteria met:
- All CRUD operations (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE)
- Complex WHERE expressions with AND/OR/NOT
- JOIN support (INNER, LEFT, RIGHT, FULL, CROSS)
- ORDER BY with multiple columns
- LIMIT/OFFSET pagination
- Aggregate functions (count, sum, avg, min, max)
- Subqueries
- SQL injection impossible (parameterized queries)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -7,10 +7,10 @@
 {"id":"sqlmodel_rust-53e.5","title":"Core: Define Model trait and FieldInfo","description":"# Task: Define Model Trait and FieldInfo

## Context
The Model trait is the core abstraction that connects Rust structs to database tables. It provides metadata about the table, columns, and type mappings. The derive macro will implement this trait.

## Current State
Basic Model trait exists. Needs enhancement for full functionality.

## Required Design

### Model Trait
```rust
/// Trait for types that can be persisted to a database.
/// 
/// This trait is typically derived using `#[derive(Model)]`.
/// It provides all metadata needed for CRUD operations.
pub trait Model: Sized + Send + Sync {
    /// Table name in the database
    const TABLE_NAME: &'static str;
    
    /// Primary key column name(s)
    /// For composite keys, comma-separated
    const PRIMARY_KEY: &'static [&'static str];
    
    /// Whether the primary key is auto-generated
    const AUTO_INCREMENT: bool;
    
    /// Get field metadata for all columns
    fn fields() -> &'static [FieldInfo];
    
    /// Get field info by name
    fn field(name: &str) -> Option<&'static FieldInfo> {
        Self::fields().iter().find(|f| f.name == name)
    }
    
    /// Convert from a database row
    fn from_row(row: &Row) -> Result<Self>;
    
    /// Convert to values for INSERT (excludes auto-increment fields)
    fn to_insert_values(&self) -> Vec<Value>;
    
    /// Convert to values for UPDATE (all fields)
    fn to_update_values(&self) -> Vec<Value>;
    
    /// Get primary key value(s) from instance
    fn primary_key_values(&self) -> Vec<Value>;
    
    /// Column names for INSERT (excludes auto-increment)
    fn insert_columns() -> &'static [&'static str];
    
    /// Column names for UPDATE
    fn update_columns() -> &'static [&'static str];
    
    /// All column names
    fn columns() -> &'static [&'static str];
}
```

### FieldInfo Structure
```rust
/// Metadata about a model field/column.
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct FieldInfo {
    /// Rust field name
    pub name: &'static str,
    
    /// Database column name (may differ from field name)
    pub column_name: &'static str,
    
    /// SQL type for this field
    pub sql_type: SqlType,
    
    /// Whether this field is nullable (Option<T>)
    pub nullable: bool,
    
    /// Whether this is part of the primary key
    pub primary_key: bool,
    
    /// Whether this field auto-increments
    pub auto_increment: bool,
    
    /// Whether this field must be unique
    pub unique: bool,
    
    /// Foreign key reference ("table.column")
    pub foreign_key: Option<&'static str>,
    
    /// Default value expression
    pub default: Option<&'static str>,
    
    /// Whether to include in SELECT
    pub selectable: bool,
    
    /// Whether to include in INSERT
    pub insertable: bool,
    
    /// Whether to include in UPDATE
    pub updatable: bool,
    
    /// Index of this field in the struct
    pub index: usize,
}
```

### SqlType Enum
```rust
/// SQL column types.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum SqlType {
    // Integers
    TinyInt,
    SmallInt,
    Integer,
    BigInt,
    
    // Floating point
    Real,
    Double,
    Numeric { precision: u8, scale: u8 },
    
    // Strings
    Char(u32),
    VarChar(u32),
    Text,
    
    // Binary
    Binary(u32),
    VarBinary(u32),
    Blob,
    
    // Date/Time
    Date,
    Time,
    DateTime,
    Timestamp,
    TimestampTz,
    
    // Boolean
    Boolean,
    
    // JSON
    Json,
    JsonB,
    
    // UUID
    Uuid,
    
    // Arrays (PostgreSQL)
    Array(Box<SqlType>),
    
    // Custom type name
    Custom(&'static str),
}

impl SqlType {
    /// Get the SQL type name for a specific dialect
    pub fn sql_name(&self, dialect: Dialect) -> &'static str;
    
    /// Default Rust type that maps to this SQL type
    pub fn default_rust_type(&self) -> &'static str;
}
```

### Type Mapping
```rust
/// Map Rust types to SQL types
pub trait ToSqlType {
    fn sql_type() -> SqlType;
}

impl ToSqlType for i32 { fn sql_type() -> SqlType { SqlType::Integer } }
impl ToSqlType for i64 { fn sql_type() -> SqlType { SqlType::BigInt } }
impl ToSqlType for String { fn sql_type() -> SqlType { SqlType::Text } }
impl ToSqlType for bool { fn sql_type() -> SqlType { SqlType::Boolean } }
impl<T: ToSqlType> ToSqlType for Option<T> { fn sql_type() -> SqlType { T::sql_type() } }
// etc.
```

## Testing Requirements
- Model trait can be implemented manually
- FieldInfo correctly represents all field attributes
- SqlType generates correct SQL for each dialect
- ToSqlType maps all common Rust types

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Model trait fully defined
- [ ] FieldInfo captures all metadata needed
- [ ] SqlType covers all common SQL types
- [ ] Dialect-aware SQL type names
- [ ] ToSqlType implemented for primitives
- [ ] Documentation with examples

## Files to Modify
- crates/sqlmodel-core/src/model.rs
- crates/sqlmodel-core/src/field.rs
- crates/sqlmodel-core/src/types.rs

## Estimated Effort
~350 lines of trait/struct definitions","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:21:24.889721049-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T11:21:24.889721049-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"sqlmodel_rust-53e.5","depends_on_id":"sqlmodel_rust-53e","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:21:24.891156703-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"sqlmodel_rust-53e.6","title":"Core: Unit tests for all core types","description":"# Task: Unit Tests for All Core Types

## Context
Comprehensive unit tests for sqlmodel-core to ensure Value, Row, Error, and Model types work correctly in all cases.

## Test Categories

### Value Tests
- Round-trip conversion: T -> Value -> T for all types
- Null handling: Value::Null conversions
- Type coercion: Valid conversions (i32 -> i64)
- Type errors: Invalid conversions return proper errors
- Edge cases: i64::MAX, f64::INFINITY, empty strings
- Display formatting for debugging
- Equality and ordering

### Row Tests
- Index access: valid and invalid indices
- Named access: valid and invalid names
- Type conversion: get_as<T> for all types
- Column iteration: names and values
- Arc sharing: verify ColumnInfo is shared
- Empty rows
- Wide rows (100+ columns)

### Error Tests
- Display formatting for all variants
- Error chaining (source)
- SQLSTATE classification
- is_retryable() correctness
- From conversions

### Model Tests
- Manual Model impl for test struct
- FieldInfo correctness
- SqlType SQL names by dialect
- ToSqlType for all primitives

## Test Infrastructure
```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    
    // Test fixtures
    fn sample_values() -> Vec<Value> { ... }
    fn sample_row() -> Row { ... }
    
    // Property-based tests with proptest
    proptest! {
        #[test]
        fn value_roundtrip_i64(v: i64) {
            let value = Value::BigInt(v);
            let back: i64 = value.try_into().unwrap();
            assert_eq!(v, back);
        }
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] >95% code coverage for value.rs
- [ ] >95% code coverage for row.rs
- [ ] >95% code coverage for error.rs
- [ ] >95% code coverage for model.rs
- [ ] Property tests for round-trip conversions
- [ ] Edge case tests documented

## Files to Create/Modify
- crates/sqlmodel-core/src/value.rs (add tests module)
- crates/sqlmodel-core/src/row.rs (add tests module)
- crates/sqlmodel-core/src/error.rs (add tests module)
- crates/sqlmodel-core/src/model.rs (add tests module)

 {"id":"sqlmodel_rust-6bs","title":"SQLModel Rust: SQLite Driver","description":"# Epic: SQLite Driver (sqlmodel-sqlite)

## Overview
This epic implements the SQLite database driver using FFI bindings to libsqlite3. SQLite is an embedded database (no network protocol), so we wrap the C library with safe Rust abstractions and integrate with asupersync.

## Rationale
SQLite is fundamentally different from PostgreSQL/MySQL:
- File-based, not client-server
- No network protocol to implement
- Uses C library via FFI
- Single-writer, multiple-reader model

We use FFI because:
- SQLite file format is complex (B-trees, pages, WAL)
- Implementing from scratch would be a database engine
- libsqlite3 is battle-tested and optimized
- FFI is the standard approach (rusqlite does this too)

## Key Components

### 1. FFI Bindings (ffi.rs)
Low-level bindings to libsqlite3:
- sqlite3_open_v2, sqlite3_close
- sqlite3_prepare_v2, sqlite3_finalize
- sqlite3_step, sqlite3_reset
- sqlite3_bind_* (parameter binding)
- sqlite3_column_* (result fetching)
- sqlite3_exec, sqlite3_changes
- sqlite3_errmsg, sqlite3_errcode

### 2. Safe Wrapper Types
- SqliteConnection: Owns sqlite3* handle
- SqliteStatement: Owns sqlite3_stmt* handle
- SqliteRow: Represents a result row
- SqliteTransaction: Manages BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK

### 3. Type Mapping
Rust to SQLite:
- i32, i64 -> INTEGER
- f32, f64 -> REAL
- String, &str -> TEXT
- Vec<u8>, &[u8] -> BLOB
- bool -> INTEGER (0/1)
- Option<T> -> NULL or T

SQLite to Rust:
- INTEGER -> i64 (then convert)
- REAL -> f64
- TEXT -> String
- BLOB -> Vec<u8>
- NULL -> Option::None

### 4. Connection Implementation
Implement Connection trait for SqliteConnection:
- query() -> Outcome<Vec<Row>, Error>
- query_one() -> Outcome<Option<Row>, Error>
- execute() -> Outcome<u64, Error>
- insert() -> Outcome<i64, Error> (last_insert_rowid)
- begin() -> Outcome<Transaction, Error>

### 5. asupersync Integration
- Wrap blocking FFI calls in spawn_blocking equivalent
- Support cancellation via periodic checks
- Budget/timeout support

## SQLite-Specific Features
- PRAGMA support for configuration
- WAL mode for concurrent reads
- Busy timeout for lock contention
- User-defined functions (future)
- Virtual tables (future)

## Key Design Decisions
1. **Use libsqlite3-sys or manual FFI**: Start with manual for control
2. **Statement caching**: Cache prepared statements for performance
3. **Connection modes**: Support read-only, read-write, create
4. **Thread safety**: SQLite has its own threading model; respect it
5. **Memory management**: Proper cleanup of C resources

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Open/close connections to SQLite files
- [ ] Execute queries with parameter binding
- [ ] Fetch results as Value/Row types
- [ ] Transaction support (begin/commit/rollback)
- [ ] Correct NULL handling
- [ ] All type conversions work correctly
- [ ] Error messages include SQLite error details
- [ ] No memory leaks (valgrind clean)
- [ ] Works with in-memory databases (:memory:)
- [ ] Concurrent read access works correctly

## Dependencies
- sqlmodel-core (Connection trait, Value, Row, Error)
- libsqlite3 (system library)
- asupersync (for async integration)

## Estimated Scope
~800 lines of FFI wrapper code","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:18:17.497953413-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T11:18:17.497953413-05:00"}
-{"id":"sqlmodel_rust-6vd","title":"SQLModel Rust: Query Builder Layer","description":"# Epic: Query Builder Layer (sqlmodel-query)

## Overview
This epic implements the type-safe SQL query builder that generates SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE statements. It provides a fluent API similar to SQLAlchemy's expression language but with compile-time type safety.

## Rationale
Python SQLModel/SQLAlchemy builds queries at runtime using method chaining. We preserve this ergonomic API while adding:
- Compile-time validation of column references
- Type-safe expression building
- Parameterized queries (SQL injection prevention)
- Dialect-aware SQL generation

## Key Components

### 1. Expression System (expr.rs)
The foundation for building WHERE clauses and computed values:
- Column references: Expr::col("name")
- Literals: Expr::lit(42), Expr::lit("hello")
- Binary ops: eq, ne, lt, gt, le, ge, like, in_list
- Logical ops: and, or, not
- Null checks: is_null, is_not_null
- Functions: count, sum, avg, min, max

### 2. SELECT Builder (select.rs)
Fluent interface for SELECT queries:
- select!(Model) macro entry point
- .filter(expr) for WHERE clauses
- .order_by(col.asc()/desc())
- .limit(n), .offset(n)
- .join(Model, on_expr) for JOINs
- .all(cx, conn) -> Vec<Model>
- .first(cx, conn) -> Option<Model>
- .count(cx, conn) -> u64

### 3. INSERT Builder (builder.rs)
- insert!(model) macro entry point
- Single row insert
- Bulk insert with .values(vec![...])
- .returning(cols) for PostgreSQL
- .execute(cx, conn) -> last_insert_id

### 4. UPDATE Builder (builder.rs)
- update!(model) for updating existing model
- .set(col, value) for partial updates
- .filter(expr) for conditional update
- .execute(cx, conn) -> rows_affected

### 5. DELETE Builder (builder.rs)
- delete!(Model) macro entry point
- .filter(expr) for conditional delete
- .execute(cx, conn) -> rows_affected

## Key Design Decisions
1. **Macro entry points**: select!/insert!/update!/delete! provide ergonomic API
2. **Builder pattern**: Each method returns Self for chaining
3. **Deferred execution**: Query only runs when .all()/.execute() called
4. **Parameterized queries**: Values passed separately, never interpolated
5. **Dialect abstraction**: SQL generation is dialect-aware (SQLite vs PostgreSQL vs MySQL)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All CRUD operations supported
- [ ] Complex WHERE expressions with AND/OR/NOT
- [ ] JOIN support (INNER, LEFT, RIGHT, FULL)
- [ ] ORDER BY with multiple columns
- [ ] LIMIT/OFFSET pagination
- [ ] Aggregate functions
- [ ] Subqueries (basic support)
- [ ] SQL injection impossible by design
- [ ] Generated SQL is readable and efficient

## Dependencies
- sqlmodel-core (Value, Row, Connection traits)

## Estimated Scope
~1200 lines of query builder code","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:17:23.82221618-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T11:17:23.82221618-05:00"}

 {"id":"sqlmodel_rust-6vd.1","title":"Query: Implement expression system","description":"# Task: Implement Expression System

## Context
The expression system is the foundation for building WHERE clauses, ORDER BY, and computed columns. It must be type-safe and generate correct SQL for each dialect.

## Expression Types

### Core Expression Enum
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub enum Expr {
    /// Column reference: "users"."name"
    Column {
        table: Option<String>,
        name: String,
    },
    
    /// Literal value: 42, 'hello', NULL
    Literal(Value),
    
    /// Binary operation: a = b, a > b, a AND b
    Binary {
        left: Box<Expr>,
        op: BinaryOp,
        right: Box<Expr>,
    },
    
    /// Unary operation: NOT a, -a, a IS NULL
    Unary {
        op: UnaryOp,
        expr: Box<Expr>,
    },
    
    /// Function call: COUNT(*), UPPER(name)
    Function {
        name: String,
        args: Vec<Expr>,
    },
    
    /// CASE WHEN ... THEN ... ELSE ... END
    Case {
        when_clauses: Vec<(Expr, Expr)>,
        else_clause: Option<Box<Expr>>,
    },
    
    /// Subquery: (SELECT ...)
    Subquery(Box<Select<()>>),
    
    /// IN list: a IN (1, 2, 3)
    InList {
        expr: Box<Expr>,
        list: Vec<Expr>,
        negated: bool,
    },
    
    /// BETWEEN: a BETWEEN 1 AND 10
    Between {
        expr: Box<Expr>,
        low: Box<Expr>,
        high: Box<Expr>,
        negated: bool,
    },
    
    /// LIKE: name LIKE '%foo%'
    Like {
        expr: Box<Expr>,
        pattern: String,
        negated: bool,
    },
    
    /// Placeholder for bound parameters: $1, ?
    Placeholder(usize),
    
    /// Raw SQL expression (escape hatch)
    Raw(String),
}
```

### Binary Operations
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
pub enum BinaryOp {
    // Comparison
    Eq,      // =
    Ne,      // != or <>
    Lt,      // <
    Le,      // <=
    Gt,      // >
    Ge,      // >=
    
    // Logical
    And,     // AND
    Or,      // OR
    
    // Arithmetic
    Add,     // +
    Sub,     // -
    Mul,     // *
    Div,     // /
    Mod,     // %
    
    // String
    Concat,  // || (PostgreSQL) or CONCAT() (MySQL)
    
    // Bitwise
    BitAnd,  // &
    BitOr,   // |
    BitXor,  // ^
}
```

### Unary Operations
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
pub enum UnaryOp {
    Not,         // NOT
    Negate,      // -
    IsNull,      // IS NULL
    IsNotNull,   // IS NOT NULL
    BitNot,      // ~
}
```

### Fluent Builder Methods
```rust
impl Expr {
    // Constructors
    pub fn col(name: &str) -> Self;
    pub fn qualified(table: &str, column: &str) -> Self;
    pub fn lit<T: Into<Value>>(value: T) -> Self;
    pub fn null() -> Self;
    
    // Comparison (return Expr for chaining)
    pub fn eq<T: Into<Expr>>(self, other: T) -> Self;
    pub fn ne<T: Into<Expr>>(self, other: T) -> Self;
    pub fn lt<T: Into<Expr>>(self, other: T) -> Self;
    pub fn le<T: Into<Expr>>(self, other: T) -> Self;
    pub fn gt<T: Into<Expr>>(self, other: T) -> Self;
    pub fn ge<T: Into<Expr>>(self, other: T) -> Self;
    
    // Logical
    pub fn and<T: Into<Expr>>(self, other: T) -> Self;
    pub fn or<T: Into<Expr>>(self, other: T) -> Self;
    pub fn not(self) -> Self;
    
    // Null checks
    pub fn is_null(self) -> Self;
    pub fn is_not_null(self) -> Self;
    
    // Containment
    pub fn in_list<T: Into<Expr>>(self, list: Vec<T>) -> Self;
    pub fn not_in<T: Into<Expr>>(self, list: Vec<T>) -> Self;
    pub fn between<T: Into<Expr>>(self, low: T, high: T) -> Self;
    pub fn not_between<T: Into<Expr>>(self, low: T, high: T) -> Self;
    
    // Pattern matching
    pub fn like(self, pattern: &str) -> Self;
    pub fn not_like(self, pattern: &str) -> Self;
    pub fn ilike(self, pattern: &str) -> Self; // Case-insensitive (PG)
    
    // Ordering (for ORDER BY)
    pub fn asc(self) -> OrderBy;
    pub fn desc(self) -> OrderBy;
    pub fn nulls_first(self) -> OrderBy;
    pub fn nulls_last(self) -> OrderBy;
}

// Aggregate functions
impl Expr {
    pub fn count() -> Self;          // COUNT(*)
    pub fn count_expr(self) -> Self; // COUNT(expr)
    pub fn sum(self) -> Self;
    pub fn avg(self) -> Self;
    pub fn min(self) -> Self;
    pub fn max(self) -> Self;
}
```

### SQL Generation
```rust
impl Expr {
    pub fn to_sql(&self, dialect: Dialect, params: &mut Vec<Value>) -> String {
        match self {
            Expr::Column { table, name } => {
                if let Some(t) = table {
                    format!("\"{}\".\"{}\"", t, name)
                } else {
                    format!("\"{}\"", name)
                }
            }
            Expr::Literal(value) => {
                params.push(value.clone());
                dialect.placeholder(params.len())
            }
            Expr::Binary { left, op, right } => {
                let l = left.to_sql(dialect, params);
                let r = right.to_sql(dialect, params);
                format!("({} {} {})", l, op.to_sql(), r)
            }
            // ... other cases
        }
    }
}
```

## Testing Requirements
- All operator combinations
- Nested expressions
- SQL generation for each dialect
- Parameter collection
- Edge cases (NULL, empty lists)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All expression types implemented
- [ ] Fluent builder API
- [ ] Correct SQL generation
- [ ] Proper parameter binding
- [ ] Dialect-specific output
- [ ] Comprehensive tests

## Files to Modify
- crates/sqlmodel-query/src/expr.rs

 {"id":"sqlmodel_rust-6vd.2","title":"Query: Implement SELECT builder with execution","description":"# Task: Implement SELECT Builder with Execution

## Context
The SELECT builder is the primary way to query data. It supports WHERE, ORDER BY, LIMIT, OFFSET, JOINs, and aggregations, all with a fluent API.

## API Design

### Basic Usage
```rust
// Type-safe SELECT returning Vec<Hero>
let heroes: Vec<Hero> = select!(Hero)
    .filter(Expr::col("age").gt(18))
    .order_by(Expr::col("name").asc())
    .limit(10)
    .all(cx, &conn)
    .await?;

// Single result
let hero: Option<Hero> = select!(Hero)
    .filter(Expr::col("id").eq(42))
    .first(cx, &conn)
    .await?;

// Count
let count: u64 = select!(Hero)
    .filter(Expr::col("team_id").is_not_null())
    .count(cx, &conn)
    .await?;
```

### Select Struct
```rust
pub struct Select<M: Model> {
    /// Columns to select (None = all columns)
    columns: Option<Vec<Expr>>,
    /// Table name (from Model)
    table: String,
    /// Table alias
    alias: Option<String>,
    /// WHERE clause
    filter: Option<Expr>,
    /// ORDER BY clauses
    order_by: Vec<OrderBy>,
    /// LIMIT
    limit: Option<u64>,
    /// OFFSET
    offset: Option<u64>,
    /// JOIN clauses
    joins: Vec<Join>,
    /// GROUP BY columns
    group_by: Vec<Expr>,
    /// HAVING clause
    having: Option<Expr>,
    /// DISTINCT
    distinct: bool,
    /// FOR UPDATE (locking)
    for_update: bool,
    /// Phantom for Model type
    _marker: PhantomData<M>,
}
```

### Builder Methods
```rust
impl<M: Model> Select<M> {
    pub fn new() -> Self;
    
    // Filtering
    pub fn filter(mut self, expr: Expr) -> Self {
        self.filter = Some(match self.filter {
            Some(existing) => existing.and(expr),
            None => expr,
        });
        self
    }
    
    // Ordering
    pub fn order_by(mut self, order: OrderBy) -> Self {
        self.order_by.push(order);
        self
    }
    
    // Pagination
    pub fn limit(mut self, n: u64) -> Self {
        self.limit = Some(n);
        self
    }
    
    pub fn offset(mut self, n: u64) -> Self {
        self.offset = Some(n);
        self
    }
    
    // JOINs
    pub fn join<J: Model>(mut self, on: Expr) -> Self {
        self.joins.push(Join::inner::<J>(on));
        self
    }
    
    pub fn left_join<J: Model>(mut self, on: Expr) -> Self {
        self.joins.push(Join::left::<J>(on));
        self
    }
    
    // Aggregation
    pub fn group_by(mut self, column: Expr) -> Self {
        self.group_by.push(column);
        self
    }
    
    pub fn having(mut self, expr: Expr) -> Self {
        self.having = Some(expr);
        self
    }
    
    // Modifiers
    pub fn distinct(mut self) -> Self {
        self.distinct = true;
        self
    }
    
    pub fn for_update(mut self) -> Self {
        self.for_update = true;
        self
    }
}
```

### Execution Methods
```rust
impl<M: Model> Select<M> {
    /// Build the SQL query and parameters
    pub fn build(&self, dialect: Dialect) -> (String, Vec<Value>) {
        let mut params = Vec::new();
        let sql = self.to_sql(dialect, &mut params);
        (sql, params)
    }
    
    /// Execute and return all matching rows
    pub async fn all<C: Connection>(
        self,
        cx: &Cx,
        conn: &C,
    ) -> Outcome<Vec<M>, Error> {
        let (sql, params) = self.build(Dialect::detect(conn));
        let rows = conn.query(cx, &sql, &params).await?;
        
        let mut results = Vec::with_capacity(rows.len());
        for row in rows {
            results.push(M::from_row(&row)?);
        }
        Outcome::Ok(results)
    }
    
    /// Execute and return first matching row
    pub async fn first<C: Connection>(
        self,
        cx: &Cx,
        conn: &C,
    ) -> Outcome<Option<M>, Error> {
        let (sql, params) = self.build(Dialect::detect(conn));
        let row = conn.query_one(cx, &sql, &params).await?;
        
        match row {
            Some(r) => Ok(Some(M::from_row(&r)?)),
            None => Ok(None),
        }
    }
    
    /// Execute and return row count
    pub async fn count<C: Connection>(
        self,
        cx: &Cx,
        conn: &C,
    ) -> Outcome<u64, Error> {
        let count_select = Select::<M> {
            columns: Some(vec![Expr::count()]),
            ..self
        };
        let (sql, params) = count_select.build(Dialect::detect(conn));
        let row = conn.query_one(cx, &sql, &params).await?
            .ok_or(Error::Query(QueryError::unexpected("COUNT returned no rows")))?;
        
        row.get_as(0)
    }
}
```

### SQL Generation
```rust
impl<M: Model> Select<M> {
    fn to_sql(&self, dialect: Dialect, params: &mut Vec<Value>) -> String {
        let mut sql = String::from("SELECT ");
        
        if self.distinct {
            sql.push_str("DISTINCT ");
        }
        
        // Columns
        match &self.columns {
            Some(cols) => {
                let col_strs: Vec<_> = cols.iter()
                    .map(|c| c.to_sql(dialect, params))
                    .collect();
                sql.push_str(&col_strs.join(", "));
            }
            None => {
                // All columns from Model
                sql.push_str(&M::columns().join(", "));
            }
        }
        
        // FROM
        sql.push_str(" FROM ");
        sql.push_str(&self.table);
        if let Some(alias) = &self.alias {
            sql.push_str(" AS ");
            sql.push_str(alias);
        }
        
        // JOINs
        for join in &self.joins {
            sql.push_str(&join.to_sql(dialect, params));
        }
        
        // WHERE
        if let Some(filter) = &self.filter {
            sql.push_str(" WHERE ");
            sql.push_str(&filter.to_sql(dialect, params));
        }
        
        // GROUP BY
        if !self.group_by.is_empty() {
            sql.push_str(" GROUP BY ");
            let groups: Vec<_> = self.group_by.iter()
                .map(|g| g.to_sql(dialect, params))
                .collect();
            sql.push_str(&groups.join(", "));
        }
        
        // HAVING
        if let Some(having) = &self.having {
            sql.push_str(" HAVING ");
            sql.push_str(&having.to_sql(dialect, params));
        }
        
        // ORDER BY
        if !self.order_by.is_empty() {
            sql.push_str(" ORDER BY ");
            let orders: Vec<_> = self.order_by.iter()
                .map(|o| o.to_sql())
                .collect();
            sql.push_str(&orders.join(", "));
        }
        
        // LIMIT
        if let Some(n) = self.limit {
            sql.push_str(&format!(" LIMIT {}", n));
        }
        
        // OFFSET
        if let Some(n) = self.offset {
            sql.push_str(&format!(" OFFSET {}", n));
        }
        
        // FOR UPDATE
        if self.for_update {
            sql.push_str(" FOR UPDATE");
        }
        
        sql
    }
}
```

-{"id":"sqlmodel_rust-6vd.3","title":"Query: Implement INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE builders","description":"# Task: Implement INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE Builders

## Context
Complete the CRUD operations with INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE builders that follow the same fluent API pattern as SELECT.

## INSERT Builder

### Usage
```rust
// Insert single model
let id = insert!(hero).execute(cx, &conn).await?;

// Insert and return the inserted row
let hero = insert!(hero).returning::<Hero>().execute(cx, &conn).await?;

// Bulk insert
let ids = insert_many!(vec![hero1, hero2, hero3]).execute(cx, &conn).await?;
```

### Implementation
```rust
pub struct InsertBuilder<M: Model> {
    model: M,
    returning: bool,
    on_conflict: Option<OnConflict>,
}

pub struct InsertManyBuilder<M: Model> {
    models: Vec<M>,
    returning: bool,
    on_conflict: Option<OnConflict>,
}

pub enum OnConflict {
    DoNothing,
    DoUpdate { columns: Vec<String> },
}

impl<M: Model> InsertBuilder<M> {
    pub fn new(model: M) -> Self;
    
    /// Return the inserted row (RETURNING * in PostgreSQL)
    pub fn returning(mut self) -> Self {
        self.returning = true;
        self
    }
    
    /// Handle conflicts (UPSERT)
    pub fn on_conflict_do_nothing(mut self) -> Self {
        self.on_conflict = Some(OnConflict::DoNothing);
        self
    }
    
    pub fn on_conflict_do_update(mut self, columns: &[&str]) -> Self {
        self.on_conflict = Some(OnConflict::DoUpdate {
            columns: columns.iter().map(|s| s.to_string()).collect(),
        });
        self
    }
    
    /// Execute and return last insert ID
    pub async fn execute<C: Connection>(
        self,
        cx: &Cx,
        conn: &C,
    ) -> Outcome<i64, Error>;
    
    fn to_sql(&self, dialect: Dialect, params: &mut Vec<Value>) -> String {
        // INSERT INTO table (col1, col2) VALUES ($1, $2)
        let columns = M::insert_columns();
        let values = self.model.to_insert_values();
        
        let mut sql = format!("INSERT INTO {} (", M::TABLE_NAME);
        sql.push_str(&columns.join(", "));
        sql.push_str(") VALUES (");
        
        let placeholders: Vec<_> = (1..=values.len())
            .map(|i| dialect.placeholder(i))
            .collect();
        sql.push_str(&placeholders.join(", "));
        sql.push_str(")");
        
        if let Some(conflict) = &self.on_conflict {
            match conflict {
                OnConflict::DoNothing => {
                    sql.push_str(" ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING");
                }
                OnConflict::DoUpdate { columns } => {
                    sql.push_str(" ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE SET ");
                    let updates: Vec<_> = columns.iter()
                        .map(|c| format!("{} = EXCLUDED.{}", c, c))
                        .collect();
                    sql.push_str(&updates.join(", "));
                }
            }
        }
        
        if self.returning {
            sql.push_str(" RETURNING *");
        }
        
        params.extend(values);
        sql
    }
}
```

## UPDATE Builder

### Usage
```rust
// Update model by primary key
let rows = update!(hero).execute(cx, &conn).await?;

// Update with explicit SET
let rows = update!(Hero)
    .set("age", 26)
    .filter(Expr::col("id").eq(42))
    .execute(cx, &conn).await?;
```

### Implementation
```rust
pub struct UpdateBuilder<M: Model> {
    model: Option<M>,
    sets: Vec<(String, Expr)>,
    filter: Option<Expr>,
    returning: bool,
}

impl<M: Model> UpdateBuilder<M> {
    /// Update a model instance (uses primary key for WHERE)
    pub fn from_model(model: M) -> Self;
    
    /// Start a new UPDATE statement
    pub fn new() -> Self;
    
    /// Set a column to a value
    pub fn set<V: Into<Expr>>(mut self, column: &str, value: V) -> Self {
        self.sets.push((column.to_string(), value.into()));
        self
    }
    
    /// Add WHERE clause
    pub fn filter(mut self, expr: Expr) -> Self {
        self.filter = Some(match self.filter {
            Some(existing) => existing.and(expr),
            None => expr,
        });
        self
    }
    
    /// Return updated rows
    pub fn returning(mut self) -> Self {
        self.returning = true;
        self
    }
    
    /// Execute and return rows affected
    pub async fn execute<C: Connection>(
        self,
        cx: &Cx,
        conn: &C,
    ) -> Outcome<u64, Error>;
    
    fn to_sql(&self, dialect: Dialect, params: &mut Vec<Value>) -> String {
        let mut sql = format!("UPDATE {} SET ", M::TABLE_NAME);
        
        if let Some(model) = &self.model {
            // Update all columns from model
            let columns = M::update_columns();
            let values = model.to_update_values();
            let pk_values = model.primary_key_values();
            
            let sets: Vec<_> = columns.iter()
                .enumerate()
                .map(|(i, col)| {
                    params.push(values[i].clone());
                    format!("{} = {}", col, dialect.placeholder(params.len()))
                })
                .collect();
            sql.push_str(&sets.join(", "));
            
            // WHERE primary key = value
            sql.push_str(" WHERE ");
            let pk_clauses: Vec<_> = M::PRIMARY_KEY.iter()
                .zip(pk_values.iter())
                .map(|(col, val)| {
                    params.push(val.clone());
                    format!("{} = {}", col, dialect.placeholder(params.len()))
                })
                .collect();
            sql.push_str(&pk_clauses.join(" AND "));
        } else {
            // Use explicit SET clauses
            let sets: Vec<_> = self.sets.iter()
                .map(|(col, expr)| {
                    format!("{} = {}", col, expr.to_sql(dialect, params))
                })
                .collect();
            sql.push_str(&sets.join(", "));
            
            if let Some(filter) = &self.filter {
                sql.push_str(" WHERE ");
                sql.push_str(&filter.to_sql(dialect, params));
            }
        }
        
        if self.returning {
            sql.push_str(" RETURNING *");
        }
        
        sql
    }
}
```

    model: M,
    returning: bool,

}

    models: Vec<M>,
    returning: bool,

    DoNothing,
    DoUpdate { columns: Vec<String> },

impl<M: Model> InsertBuilder<M> {
    pub fn new(model: M) -> Self;
    
    /// Return the inserted row (RETURNING * in PostgreSQL)
    pub fn returning(mut self) -> Self {
        self.returning = true;
        self
    }
    
    /// Handle conflicts (UPSERT)
    pub fn on_conflict_do_nothing(mut self) -> Self {
        self.on_conflict = Some(OnConflict::DoNothing);
        self
    }
    
    pub fn on_conflict_do_update(mut self, columns: &[&str]) -> Self {
        self.on_conflict = Some(OnConflict::DoUpdate {
            columns: columns.iter().map(|s| s.to_string()).collect(),
        });
        self
    }
    
    /// Execute and return last insert ID
    pub async fn execute<C: Connection>(
        self,
        cx: &Cx,
        conn: &C,
    ) -> Outcome<i64, Error>;
    
    fn to_sql(&self, dialect: Dialect, params: &mut Vec<Value>) -> String {
        // INSERT INTO table (col1, col2) VALUES ($1, $2)
        let columns = M::insert_columns();
        let values = self.model.to_insert_values();
        
        let mut sql = format!("INSERT INTO {} (", M::TABLE_NAME);
        sql.push_str(&columns.join(", "));
        sql.push_str(") VALUES (");
        
        let placeholders: Vec<_> = (1..=values.len())
            .map(|i| dialect.placeholder(i))
            .collect();
        sql.push_str(&placeholders.join(", "));
        sql.push_str(")");
        
        if let Some(conflict) = &self.on_conflict {
            match conflict {
                OnConflict::DoNothing => {
                    sql.push_str(" ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING");
                }
                OnConflict::DoUpdate { columns } => {
                    sql.push_str(" ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE SET ");
                    let updates: Vec<_> = columns.iter()
                        .map(|c| format!("{} = EXCLUDED.{}", c, c))
                        .collect();
                    sql.push_str(&updates.join(", "));
                }
            }
        }
        
        if self.returning {
            sql.push_str(" RETURNING *");
        }
        
        params.extend(values);
        sql
    }
}

## UPDATE Builder

### Usage
```rust
// Update model by primary key
let rows = update!(hero).execute(cx, &conn).await?;

// Update with explicit SET
let rows = update!(Hero)
    .set("age", 26)
    .filter(Expr::col("id").eq(42))
    .execute(cx, &conn).await?;
```

### Implementation
```rust
pub struct UpdateBuilder<M: Model> {
    model: Option<M>,
    sets: Vec<(String, Expr)>,
    filter: Option<Expr>,
    returning: bool,
}

impl<M: Model> UpdateBuilder<M> {
    /// Update a model instance (uses primary key for WHERE)
    pub fn from_model(model: M) -> Self;
    
    /// Start a new UPDATE statement
    pub fn new() -> Self;
    
    /// Set a column to a value
    pub fn set<V: Into<Expr>>(mut self, column: &str, value: V) -> Self {
        self.sets.push((column.to_string(), value.into()));
        self
    }
    
    /// Add WHERE clause
    pub fn filter(mut self, expr: Expr) -> Self {
        self.filter = Some(match self.filter {
            Some(existing) => existing.and(expr),
            None => expr,
        });
        self
    }
    
    /// Return updated rows
    pub fn returning(mut self) -> Self {
        self.returning = true;
        self
    }
    
    /// Execute and return rows affected
    pub async fn execute<C: Connection>(
        self,
        cx: &Cx,
        conn: &C,
    ) -> Outcome<u64, Error>;
    
    fn to_sql(&self, dialect: Dialect, params: &mut Vec<Value>) -> String {
        let mut sql = format!("UPDATE {} SET ", M::TABLE_NAME);
        
        if let Some(model) = &self.model {
            // Update all columns from model
            let columns = M::update_columns();
            let values = model.to_update_values();
            let pk_values = model.primary_key_values();
            
            let sets: Vec<_> = columns.iter()
                .enumerate()
                .map(|(i, col)| {
                    params.push(values[i].clone());
                    format!("{} = {}", col, dialect.placeholder(params.len()))
                })
                .collect();
            sql.push_str(&sets.join(", "));
            
            // WHERE primary key = value
            sql.push_str(" WHERE ");
            let pk_clauses: Vec<_> = M::PRIMARY_KEY.iter()
                .zip(pk_values.iter())
                .map(|(col, val)| {
                    params.push(val.clone());
                    format!("{} = {}", col, dialect.placeholder(params.len()))
                })
                .collect();
            sql.push_str(&pk_clauses.join(" AND "));
        } else {
            // Use explicit SET clauses
            let sets: Vec<_> = self.sets.iter()
                .map(|(col, expr)| {
                    format!("{} = {}", col, expr.to_sql(dialect, params))
                })
                .collect();
            sql.push_str(&sets.join(", "));
            
            if let Some(filter) = &self.filter {
                sql.push_str(" WHERE ");
                sql.push_str(&filter.to_sql(dialect, params));
            }
        }
        
        if self.returning {
            sql.push_str(" RETURNING *");
        }
        
        sql
    }
}
```

- [ ] On conflict (UPSERT) works

 {"id":"sqlmodel_rust-84w","title":"SQLModel Rust: Testing Infrastructure","description":"# Epic: Testing Infrastructure

## Overview
This epic establishes comprehensive testing infrastructure for SQLModel Rust, including unit tests, integration tests, end-to-end tests, and performance benchmarks. All tests use asupersync's LabRuntime for deterministic async testing.

## Rationale
Thorough testing is critical for a database library because:
- SQL generation must be correct for all edge cases
- Type conversions must be lossless and reversible
- Protocol implementations must handle all message types
- Concurrent access must be safe
- Performance regressions must be caught

## Key Components

### 1. Unit Test Framework
Per-crate unit tests:
- sqlmodel-core: Value conversions, Row operations, Error formatting
- sqlmodel-macros: Proc macro output verification
- sqlmodel-query: SQL generation correctness
- sqlmodel-schema: DDL generation, migration logic
- sqlmodel-pool: Pool state management

### 2. Integration Test Fixtures
Test database setup:
- SQLite in-memory databases
- PostgreSQL Docker container
- MySQL Docker container
- Schema creation/teardown
- Test data seeding

### 3. Protocol Tests
For PostgreSQL and MySQL:
- Message parsing tests (binary fixtures)
- Message serialization tests
- Authentication flow tests (mock server)
- Error handling tests
- Edge cases (large messages, binary data)

### 4. CRUD Test Suite
Standard operations across all drivers:
- INSERT single row, multiple rows
- SELECT with filters, joins, ordering
- UPDATE with conditions
- DELETE with conditions
- Transaction commit/rollback
- NULL handling
- Type round-trips

### 5. Concurrency Tests
Stress testing:
- Concurrent queries on same connection
- Connection pool under load
- Transaction isolation verification
- Deadlock detection/handling

### 6. E2E Test Scenarios
Real-world usage patterns:
- Blog application (users, posts, comments)
- E-commerce (products, orders, inventory)
- Full CRUD lifecycle
- Migration up/down/up cycle
- Multi-tenant isolation

### 7. Performance Benchmarks
Using criterion.rs:
- Query building latency
- Small query execution
- Bulk insert performance
- Connection pool throughput
- Comparison with raw driver performance

### 8. Regression Tests
Prevent regressions:
- Known bug reproductions
- Edge cases discovered in production
- Type coercion edge cases
- SQL dialect differences

### 9. Logging and Diagnostics
Test output quality:
- Detailed logging at TRACE level
- Query timing information
- Connection state logging
- Failure diagnostics (query, params, error)

## Test Organization
```
tests/
├── unit/
│   ├── core/
│   ├── macros/
│   ├── query/
│   ├── schema/
│   └── pool/
├── integration/
│   ├── sqlite/
│   ├── postgres/
│   └── mysql/
├── e2e/
│   ├── blog_app/
│   └── ecommerce/
├── protocol/
│   ├── postgres_messages/
│   └── mysql_messages/
├── fixtures/
│   ├── schemas/
│   └── data/
└── benches/
    ├── query_building.rs
    └── execution.rs
```

## Key Design Decisions
1. **LabRuntime for async**: Deterministic, reproducible async tests
2. **Docker for real databases**: Test against actual PostgreSQL/MySQL
3. **In-memory SQLite for speed**: Fast unit tests
4. **Fixture-based protocol tests**: Binary message fixtures for parsing
5. **Property-based testing**: Use proptest for type conversions

## Success Criteria
- [ ] >90% code coverage
- [ ] All public APIs have doc tests
- [ ] Integration tests for all CRUD operations
- [ ] Protocol tests cover all message types
- [ ] E2E tests validate real usage patterns
- [ ] Benchmarks establish performance baselines
- [ ] CI runs all tests on every commit
- [ ] Test failures have clear diagnostic output

## Dependencies
- All sqlmodel-* crates
- asupersync (LabRuntime)
- criterion (benchmarks)
- proptest (property testing)
- testcontainers (Docker)

## Estimated Scope
~3000 lines of test code","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:19:38.373062215-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T11:19:38.373062215-05:00"}

 {"id":"sqlmodel_rust-b0q","title":"SQLModel Rust: PostgreSQL Protocol Implementation","description":"# Epic: PostgreSQL Protocol Implementation (sqlmodel-postgres)

## Overview
This epic implements the PostgreSQL wire protocol from scratch. PostgreSQL uses a well-documented TCP-based protocol with message framing. We implement this protocol directly using asupersync's TCP primitives for full control and native async integration.

## Rationale
Why implement the protocol ourselves instead of using libpq or tokio-postgres?
1. **Full async control**: Native asupersync integration, not adapter patterns
2. **Cancellation support**: Protocol-level query cancellation
3. **Zero-copy potential**: Control over buffer management
4. **Understanding**: Know exactly what's happening on the wire
5. **First principles**: No hidden complexity or surprising behaviors

## Key Components

### 1. Message Protocol (protocol.rs)
PostgreSQL uses length-prefixed messages:
- First byte: message type (or none for startup)
- Next 4 bytes: length (including self)
- Remaining: payload

Message types (frontend -> backend):
- Startup: Version, parameters
- Query: Simple query string
- Parse: Prepare statement
- Bind: Bind parameters
- Describe: Get row description
- Execute: Run prepared
- Sync: Synchronize
- Terminate: Close connection
- CancelRequest: Cancel running query
- CopyData, CopyDone, CopyFail

Message types (backend -> frontend):
- AuthenticationXxx: Auth requirements
- ParameterStatus: Server config
- BackendKeyData: Cancellation key
- ReadyForQuery: Transaction status
- RowDescription: Column metadata
- DataRow: Result row
- CommandComplete: Query done
- ErrorResponse: Error details
- NoticeResponse: Warnings
- ParseComplete, BindComplete, etc.

### 2. Authentication (auth.rs)
Support multiple auth methods:
- AuthenticationOk (trust)
- AuthenticationCleartextPassword
- AuthenticationMD5Password (md5(md5(password+user)+salt))
- AuthenticationSASL (SCRAM-SHA-256)

SCRAM-SHA-256 implementation:
- Client-first message
- Server-first message (salt, iterations)
- Client-final message (proof)
- Server-final message (verifier)

### 3. Simple Query Protocol (simple.rs)
For simple one-off queries:
- Send Query message with SQL string
- Receive: RowDescription, DataRow*, CommandComplete, ReadyForQuery
- Returns results as strings (text format)

### 4. Extended Query Protocol (extended.rs)
For prepared statements and binary data:
- Parse: Prepare named statement
- Bind: Bind parameters to statement
- Describe: Get result column info
- Execute: Run with row limit
- Sync: Flush and get ReadyForQuery

Benefits:
- Binary parameter/result format
- Statement caching
- Partial result fetching

### 5. Type System (types.rs)
PostgreSQL OID-based type system:
- Map OIDs to Rust types
- Text format encoding/decoding
- Binary format encoding/decoding
- Array type support
- Composite types (future)
- Custom types via registry

Common OIDs:
- 16: bool
- 21: int2
- 23: int4
- 20: int8
- 700: float4
- 701: float8
- 25: text
- 17: bytea
- 1082: date
- 1114: timestamp
- 2950: uuid
- 3802: jsonb

### 6. Connection Management (connection.rs)
- TCP connection establishment
- SSL/TLS upgrade (optional)
- Startup message with parameters
- Authentication handshake
- Parameter status tracking
- Ready state management
- Graceful termination

### 7. Error Handling (error.rs)
Parse ErrorResponse fields:
- Severity (ERROR, FATAL, PANIC)
- SQLSTATE code (e.g., 23505 for unique violation)
- Message, Detail, Hint
- Position, Internal position
- Schema, Table, Column, Constraint
- File, Line, Routine

### 8. Transactions
PostgreSQL transaction modes:
- BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK
- Savepoints: SAVEPOINT, ROLLBACK TO, RELEASE
- Isolation levels: READ COMMITTED, REPEATABLE READ, SERIALIZABLE
- Read-only mode

### 9. Query Cancellation
Protocol-level cancellation:
- Open new connection
- Send CancelRequest with backend key
- Original query receives cancellation

## Key Design Decisions
1. **Async from ground up**: Use asupersync's TCP, not sync + spawn_blocking
2. **Connection struct**: Owns TCP stream, manages state machine
3. **Buffer management**: Reusable read/write buffers
4. **Statement cache**: LRU cache of prepared statements
5. **Type registry**: Extensible OID -> type mapping

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Connect to PostgreSQL server
- [ ] All authentication methods work
- [ ] Simple queries execute correctly
- [ ] Extended protocol with parameters
- [ ] Binary format for common types
- [ ] Transactions with isolation levels
- [ ] Query cancellation works
- [ ] Error messages include SQLSTATE
- [ ] SSL/TLS connections
- [ ] Connection pooling integration
- [ ] COPY protocol (import/export)
- [ ] Notifications/LISTEN (future)

## Dependencies
- sqlmodel-core (Connection trait, Value, Row, Error)
- asupersync (TCP, cancellation, Budget)

## Estimated Scope
~2500 lines of protocol implementation

## References
- PostgreSQL Protocol Documentation: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/protocol.html
- SCRAM RFC: https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5802","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:18:47.384603066-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T11:18:47.384603066-05:00"}
